hints.to claim: 'to claim' is a later English derivative — 'clamamus' comes from 'clamare' (to cry out)
hints.to shout: 'to shout' is 'exclamare' — 'clamamus' comes from 'clamare' (to cry out/call)

hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive 1st pl. would be 'clamemus' — 'clamamus' has the indicative -a- stem
hints.Perfect indicative: Perfect 1st pl. would be 'clamavimus' — 'clamamus' is present tense
hints.Imperfect indicative: Imperfect 1st pl. would be 'clamabamus' — 'clamamus' is present tense

hints.claudo, claudere, clausi, clausum: These are the principal parts of 'claudere' (to close)
hints.exclamo, exclamare, exclamavi, exclamatu: These are the principal parts of 'exclamare' (to shout out)
hints.voco, vocare, vocavi, vocatum: These are the principal parts of 'vocare' (to call)

hints.Genitive: Genitive singular of 'exsul' would be 'exsulis' — 'exsules' is the nominative plural
hints.Accusative: While 'exsules' could be accusative plural, here it is nominative in apposition to the subject 'we'
hints.Vocative: Vocative would be the same form, but 'exsules' describes the subject 'we' (clamamus)

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'exsulis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'exsulis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'exsulis' ends in -is

hints.Genitive: While 'filii' could be genitive singular, here it is nominative plural in apposition to 'exsules'
hints.Dative: Dative of 'filius' would be 'filio' — 'filii' is nominative plural
hints.Vocative: Vocative plural would also be 'filii', but here it is nominative describing the subject

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'filii' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'filii' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'filii' ends in -i

hints.Nominative: Nominative is 'Eva' — 'Evae' with -ae ending is genitive singular
hints.Dative: While 'Evae' could be dative, here it is genitive modifying 'filii' (children of Eve)
hints.Vocative: Vocative of 'Eva' would be 'Eva' — 'Evae' is genitive

translation: To you we cry out, exiled children of Eve
